title: The WASM ABI and value marshalling
description: WASM functions natively pass only numeric types; strings, structs, and arrays must be encoded into linear memory and described by pointer+length.
body: Use when designing or debugging the interop boundary. Core WebAssembly function signatures carry only i32/i64/f32/f64 (and references in newer proposals); there is no native string or struct. To pass a string or array you write its bytes into linear memory and pass a pointer (offset) and length, and the other side reads them back with the matching encoding (e.g. UTF-8). Binding generators like wasm-bindgen automate this marshalling and the paired allocation/free. Do not pass complex values as a single integer or assume the receiver knows the length. Actions: use generated bindings, or pass explicit pointer+length with a fixed encoding and ownership rule. Validation signals include round-trip equality for strings/arrays across the boundary. Risk boundary: a length or encoding mismatch reads adjacent memory as data, a silent corruption.
